title: Contributor profiles stay local git intelligence
summary: Kage contributors should borrow Repowise style contributor profiles using only local git history and the existing code graph: commits, recent activity, touched files/modules, primary owned files, ownership silos, hotspot
body: Kage contributors should borrow Repowise-style contributor profiles using only local git history and the existing code graph: commits, recent activity, touched files/modules, primary-owned files, ownership silos, hotspot ownership, and commit category mix. Expose it through CLI, MCP, viewer reports, and docs without contacting GitHub or adding a hosted database.

context.fact: Kage contributors should borrow Repowise-style contributor profiles using only local git history and the existing code graph: commits, recent activity, touched files/modules, primary-owned files, ownership silos, hotspot ownership, and commit category mix.
